item.name: Alice Skateboard
item.materialsAndDetails: Supreme skate deck with natural veneer and black top ply. Multicolor 8.375 x 32.125 in. deck with original artwork by John John Jesse.

observations.details: Natural veneer deck with black top ply, printed bottom graphic, printed World Famous and box logo on top, and original artwork by John John Jesse.

signals: Skate deck category has collector base

observations.context: Supreme Week 10 included the Alice Skateboard alongside Ghostface capsule items, Dr. Martens, and other apparel/accessories.

observations.interpretation: StockX exposes a single Multicolor/OS market for the deck; the app's Graphic row maps directly to this listing.

dataQualityWarnings: StockX currently exposes the target as one Multicolor/OS listing, not separate Black and Graphic products.
The Black row is modeled for app color coverage from the same product facts and is not a separate StockX market.
No completed-sale history was captured for the Alice deck in this pass.
The live bid/ask spread is tight enough to support a day-1 call, but the market is still early and can compress after wider delivery.
No eBay completed-sale integration was used for this first pass.

model.name: transparent comparable-weighted baseline
model.target: expected resale sale price before seller fees by horizon

Black.liveOverrideNote: StockX does not expose a separate Black market for the Alice deck; Black is modeled from the Multicolor/Graphic listing for app color coverage.

method.detailedReasoning: The Alice Skateboard has a clean official product anchor: $72 retail, John John Jesse artwork, and standard Supreme deck sizing. StockX currently has one canonical Multicolor/OS listing with active bid support: $131 lowest ask, $115 highest bid, $130 sell-faster, and $143 earn-more. That supports a day-1 cop call for the Graphic app row because even after a 12% fee and $18 handling assumption, expected profit is about $36. The Black app row is not a separate resale market; Supreme's official copy describes a black top ply, while the sellable StockX product is Multicolor. Black is therefore modeled as a conservative display row, not a distinct product arbitrage.
reviewQuestionsForCritic: Should the app collapse Black and Graphic into a single Multicolor display color for this item?
Does the $16 bid/ask spread justify a stronger confidence score despite no completed-sale capture?
Will wider delivery push the deck toward retail-plus-shipping after early buyers fill?
Does John John Jesse artwork create enough collector demand for one-year appreciation above day-1 levels?
Should handling be higher than $18 for skate decks because of box size and damage risk?

Graphic.basis: Direct StockX live market anchor for the Multicolor deck listing; this is the true sellable color in marketplace data.

Black.basis: Modeled from official black top-ply product detail and app color taxonomy, discounted versus Graphic because there is no separate StockX Black product.

horizonLogic.oneWeek: Fade slightly from day-1 because wider delivery usually pressures current-season deck asks, but keep above retail because active bid support is already over $100.
horizonLogic.oneMonth: Model a further short-term compression as impatient sellers undercut, while preserving modest profit for the true Graphic/Multicolor row.
horizonLogic.oneYear: Allow collector recovery for the artwork-led deck after supply leaves the market, with Graphic outperforming the modeled Black row.

liveMarketInterpretation.interpretation: Unlike the no-bid accessory items, Alice has an actionable live market: one OS listing, active bid, sell-faster and earn-more values, and a $16 spread. The weakness is taxonomy, not liquidity: StockX treats the deck as Multicolor while the app lists Black and Graphic.

confidenceAdjustment.basis: Confidence is modestly above the base because the official retail/source facts and StockX scaffold agree, but capped because completed sales and a true separate Black market are missing.

day1Predictions.liquidityNote: Active StockX bid and tight early spread support a real day-1 exit, but watch post-delivery ask compression.
No separate StockX Black listing was found; this row exists for app color coverage and should be treated as the same deck, discounted for taxonomy risk.
day1Predictions.analystOverrideReason: Modeled below the StockX-backed Graphic/Multicolor row because Black is an app display color/top-ply detail, not an observed separate resale market.

horizonPredictions.liquidityNote: Best target: true StockX-backed Multicolor/Graphic deck with live bid support and collector-artwork upside.
Modeled color row only; use the Graphic/Multicolor market as the real resale listing.

colorLevelSummaries.reasoning: Graphic maps to the StockX Multicolor OS product. The day-1 scaffold is supported by $143 earn-more, $130 sell-faster, and a $115 bid against $72 retail. The spread is reasonable for a fresh deck, so this is a cop if acquired at retail.

Black is included because the app droplist has a Black color and Supreme describes a black top ply. There is no separate Black StockX product, so it should not be treated as a distinct resale target. Modeled value stays profitable but gets a maybe call due taxonomy risk.

avoidForResale: Black OS if it is being treated as a separate resale SKU instead of the same Multicolor deck
overall.baseRecommendation: Cop the StockX-backed Graphic/Multicolor Alice Skateboard at retail; treat Black as a taxonomy/display row rather than a separate target.
keyRisks: StockX has one Multicolor/OS listing, while the app lists Black and Graphic.
No completed-sale history was captured for the Alice Skateboard in this pass.
Fresh current-season decks often compress after delivery as undercutting increases.
Skate decks can carry higher shipping, packaging, and damage risk than small accessories.
Collector premium depends on John John Jesse artwork demand holding beyond drop-day hype.
A one-year appreciation thesis may take patience and cleaner storage condition than a quick StockX flip.
No eBay completed-sale integration was used for this first pass.
